mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: S.A.M.P.L.E. (Simple Attributes: Measuring Population Likelihood Estimates)

mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 2
mnemonics.content: Populations have Attributes, Samples give Proportions (P.A.S.P.)

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: Simple sampling of attributes involves drawing a sample from a population to estimate the proportion of items with a specific characteristic.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: The sample proportion (p̂) is an unbiased estimator for the true population proportion (p).

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: The variability of the estimate decreases as the sample size increases, following the principles of the binomial distribution.
